comment.body:
I agree that we will tag those levels. 670 is a strong possibility for tagging and will match SPX level of 6720 called out by Alma as upper bound. Though I am skeptical market will care about PCE or Powell speaking this week. 

If the fed meeting, jobs data or a million other things since April did not spook the market, no reason for it to care about PCE in late September. 

It will just correct through time and might go flat for an entire month after tagging 670 and will burn premium for put holders.

comment.body:
1) sometimes i would say, it is "Le fabuleux destin d'Amélie Poulan", sometimes i say it is "as good as it gets", and sometimes it is "Saint Anthony: the miracle worker of Padua" (2002) for personal reasons... but every movie is different... but these two has personal meanings for me. just like Woody Allen's "Midnight in Paris" for example :))

2) again, i would say Mozart "Le nozze di Figaro" and his full lifework... but also Rachmaninoff's "3rd piano concerto" (especially the final movement), or "Monteverdi - Agnusdei", but sometimes it is Schoenberg, and also Beatles, Queen, Pink Floyd, Yes "owner of a lonely heart", Bill Evans, Miles Davis, Chet Baker etc. :))

3) :DDD in my childhood... the long-haired Freddie Mercury (like as he looks in the "Killer queen" video or in the "bohemian rhapsody" originial video... also I had a male friend in Paris who was a drag queen. He wasn't gay, he just liked to dress as a woman sometimes. He looked actually beautiful as a drag queen, little touch of makeup only, but no love :))

comment.body:
1) and 3) are covered in the posts, 4) partly...

2) to be honest, i don't expect. What i see is that volatility remains under control. You know, even tho WS has a direct channel to effect volatility, still too high convexity and too hgih levels cause uncertainity. Big money doesn't want uncertainity. thats the left-tail event, tehy can't control.
20-25 VIX at max ,as it looks now. 
4) Markets would punish obvious manipulation by term premia and dollar risk premium, so even if they would have full control, they would need to play it smart. Disbalances must be rebalanced. Always. The risk is confidence slippage, not calendar slippage
5) yepp, but conditional, and more like an 'episodic spread stress" rather than any imminent euro breakup. Bcs ECB’s TPI + PEPP reinvestments are circuit breakers against disorderly fragmentation that matters. The horizont is 1-2 year.

comment.body:
Few questions! Thanks in advance for all the alpha you provide, and any answers you give to these questions. Please know I respect your time enough to not expect an answer to each of these, and acknowledge that these questions might not be optimal. I am thankful for any response in any capacity. 

1) what catalysts are you looking for (and on what time horizon) to trigger the correction down to ~6100/6000? Or if not, 
is the correction/reversion to mean  all mechanical? 
2) is it reasonable to expect a VIX climb to 20-30 on the correction, whenever it occurs? Or is it possible they could “walk it down” and keep vol suppressed <20? 
3) how long is your window of risk, and what do you use to determine if there’s a continuation or the bottom is in? 
4) (more macro and perhaps conspiracy theory) with Trump appointing new BLS advisors, do you think the amount of potential catalysts are reduced with Trump “cooking the books”? Narrative seems to be “bullish all the time under this admin, we will never get a bearish print again, esp. with earnings no longer being quarterly and him stacking the Fe 
5) is European debt crisis concerns valid? If so, on what time horizon? 

TL;DR I’m struggling to identify what the market would recognize as a valid catalyst to trigger the reversion to mean/~10% correction, or full on “crash” (20%+) and don’t have confidence to know (or what to look at) to determine when the correction (whenever it occurs) is bottoming, or if it would keep going. 

I have December Vega puts on a bet that something “happens” between now and then, but don’t know what to look for to inform me when the bottom is in (if we do decline meaningfully in that time, and if we don’t, I roll) 

Thank you again for your time, hope you have a good weekend .

comment.body:
there will be no crash now. 
pullbacks needed. 
bcs the risk must be tempered from time to time. 
time correction can't rebalance everything, and that would take too much time, and ppl don't like stagnant markets.
for me the play would rather a long short-term vol and short long-term vol. Bcs long vanna is building back on the long-term horizont, while market is keep being pushed on short vanna on the short-term tenors. 

inflationary pressures and corruption, institution's credibility is a big sentiment risk

comment.body:
NDX drop :)) not SPX
you can't just convert NDX numbers into SPX ones.
in the post, you see a %, you can extract that from the closing price
but it is NDX that matters, not SPX, bcs that leads the momentum.
If NDX breaks it, SPX will follow no matter where it trades. 
this is it.

comment.body:
i have realized profits to buy into a pullback, yes... with confidence but modestly... knowing how the last Trump market ended, and expecting that cycle in the 28/30 window...
 Gold/silver, yields, SPX up, thats the main play.
that GOLD price target is not a valid target from now, as we went from there. just look at the structure. it touches soon the resistance. below that, there is the zone where it rangebounded.
it is not like, i would buy only there... but thats the level that would still hold the structure. I confidently accumulate until those targets hold. and they will hold.
 don't predict %s in GOLD pullback, just a target range

comment.body: Hi Alma, I left a question on your ai bubble post about your market correction levels (6-10%) for SPx and Gold. Would be lovely if you could have a look at that one:) In any case, if the risk window is open now, what's the best portfolio strategy in your opinion? Going cash to buy the dip? Is your model still saying 6-10% on spx, but a bigger dip on Gold? I was curious why the dip would be stronger in Gold...
